label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what Robin learned from the story. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the story. Robin's family was at a restaurant when two homeless boys came in. The boys were dirty and smelled, but they were just looking for a cup of coffee to warm up. His mother saw them, ordered some food for them, and gave it to them without expecting anything in return. Robin realized that this was unconditional love. Now he saves his money to help homeless people. So the main point here is about Robin's mother acting kindly towards the homeless boys without expecting anything back. Robin learned that kind treatment from his mother and now he does similar things. Looking at the options: A) Treat others kindly. That fits because the story emphasizes kindness without expecting anything. B) Don't laugh at others. The story doesn't mention laughing; it's more about showing kindness to those in need. So B is probably not right. C) Save pocket money. While Robin does save his money, the main lesson isn't about saving money but about the act of helping others. The saving is a result of the lesson, not the lesson itself. D) Don't receive
